revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Iko-no-hayamitama Hime-no-jinja]][[ja:伊古乃速御玉比売神社]] {{Expand Japanese|伊古乃速御玉比売神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Iko-no-hayamitama Hime-no Shrine | native_name = 伊古乃速御玉比売神社 | image = Iko-jinja.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Iko-no-hayamitama Hime-no Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.074083333333334|139.33972222222224}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Iko-no-hayamitama Hime-no Shrine'''|伊古乃速御玉比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} * {{ill|Q135419779|ltq=Q135419779|qid=Q135419779|1=Shikinai Supershrine}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 42 ** P155: {{ill|Q59282644|ltq=Q59282644|qid=Q59282644|1=Takagi Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q68537489|ltq=Q68537489|qid=Q68537489|1=Mika Shrine (Misato town, Saitama)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Namegawa, Saitama|lt=Namegawa|ar|ناميغاوا|arz|ناميجاوا|ceb|Namegawa-machi|de|Namegawa (Saitama)|en|Namegawa, Saitama|es|Namegawa|fa|نامه‌گاوا، سایتاما|fr|Namegawa|id|Namegawa, Saitama|it|Namegawa|ja|滑川町|ko|나메가와정|ms|Namegawa, Saitama|my|နမဲဂါဝါမြို့၊ ဆိုင်းတမ|pl|Namegawa|ru|Намегава|sv|Namegawa|tl|Namegawa, Saitama|tr|Namegawa|uk|Намеґава (Сайтама)|zh|滑川町|qid=Q1346985|1=Namegawa, Saitama}} * {{ill|Q907398|ltq=Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} * {{ill|Q1075201|ltq=Q1075201|qid=Q1075201|1=Hiki District, Saitama}} == coordinate University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いこのはやみたまひめじんじゃhttps://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == postal code (P281) == * 355-0806https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3030005011367https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Commons category (P373) == * Iko-no-hayamitama Hime-no-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Iko-no-hayamitama Hime-no Shrine former site|lt=Iko-no-hayamitama Hime-no Shrine former site|qid=Q135193399|1=Iko-no-hayamitama Hime-no Shrine former site}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県比企郡滑川町伊古1242', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Iko-no-hayamitama Hime-no-jinja}} [[JA:伊古乃速御玉比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q67200466}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Iko no Hayamitamahime Shrine |Image = [[File:Iko-jinja.jpg|250px|Iko no Hayamitamahime Shrine]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hiki District|Q1075201|qid=Q1075201|1=Hiki District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Namegawa Town|Q1346985|qid=Q1346985|1=Namegawa, Saitama}} Iko 1242 |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates 2-tier degrees minutes seconds|36|4|26.7|N|139|20|23|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto
Ōtomowake no Mikoto
Takenouchi no Sukune |Sacred object = Ninomiyama |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninken|Q357266|qid=Q357266|1=Emperor Ninken}} (449–460) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Alternate name = Awazu Myōjin |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = October 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=4|lat_sec=26.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=20|lon_sec=23|label=Iko no Hayamitamahime Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|36|4|26.7|N|139|20|23|E}}}} }} '''Iko no Hayamitamahime Shrine''' (いこのはやみたまひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Namegawa River (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11565004|qid=Q11565004|lt=Namegawa|1=Namegawa}} in {{ill|lt=Namegawa Town|Q1346985|qid=Q1346985|1=Namegawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Hiki District|Q1075201|qid=Q1075201|1=Hiki District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Ōtomowake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}. The original enshrined deity, as indicated by the shrine’s name, is Hayamitamahime no Mikoto. It is one of the candidate shrines for the minor {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=Minor Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} {{ill|lt=Chichibu District|Q1145581|qid=Q1145581|1=Chichibu District, Saitama}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === === Overview === '''Iko no Hayamitamahime Shrine (Okumiya)''' It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninken|Q357266|qid=Q357266|1=Emperor Ninken}} (449–460) by descendants of {{ill|lt=Soga no Ishikawa|Q11625205|qid=Q11625205|lt=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}} on the summit of Mount Ninomiyama. From the shrine notice board In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 4 (1576), the shrine was relocated to its present location, and the shrine on Mount Ninomiyama became the Okumiya (inner shrine). In addition, Haruna Shrine was enshrined together at the Okumiya in prayers for abundant harvests. File:Iko-jinja okumiya.jpg|Shrine building File:Iko-jinja okumiya Hachidairyuujin.jpg|Eight Great Dragon Deities '''Iko no Hayamitamahime Shrine (Hongū)''' The main hall of the Hongū enshrines Inari Ōkami and Atago Ōkami together as auxiliary deities {{sfn|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki}}. This shrine was also known by the alternate name Awazu Myōjin. The “su” (洲) of Awazu Myōjin is thought to mean “island” in Saitama Prefecture, and it is believed that a divided spirit of {{ill|lt=Awashima Myōjin|Q1575740|qid=Q1575740|lt=Awashima deity|1=Awashima-no-Kami}}, the enshrined deity of Kada Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Awashima Shrine|Q7007362|qid=Q7007362|1=Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)}}) located at Kada-no-ura in {{ill|lt=Kii Province|Q864053|qid=Q864053|1=Kii Province}} {{ill|lt=Kaiso District|Q1130990|qid=Q1130990|1=Kaisō District, Wakayama}}, was transferred here. From “Shrines of Saitama: Ōsato, Kita-Katsushika, and Hiki” (Publisher: Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines, July 1992) '''Shrines of the Awazu Myōjin lineage''' * Tsuji-shio, Namegawa Town – Awazu ShrineFrom the shrine notice board of Tsuchishio Awazu Shrine (Created March 2008 by Namegawa Town Tourism Association and Namegawa Town Board of Education) : (Enshrined deity) Hayamitamahime no Mikoto : (Founded) {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|qid=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} 3 (1783), July 3 * Fukuda (Kamifukuda), Namegawa Town – Awazu ShrineFrom the shrine notice board of Fukuda Awazu Shrine (Created March 2003 by Namegawa Town Tourism Association and Namegawa Town Board of Education) : (Enshrined deity) {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} : (Founded) {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|qid=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 2 (1395) * Yamada (Kamiyamada), Namegawa Town – Awazu ShrineFrom the shrine notice board of Yamada Awazu Shrine (Created March 2003 by Namegawa Town Tourism Association and Namegawa Town Board of Education) : (Enshrined deities) {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} : (Founded) {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|qid=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 2 (1395) * Yamada (Shimoyamada), Namegawa Town – Ōrai Awazu ShrineFrom the shrine notice board of Ōrai Awazu Shrine (Created December 1979 by Namegawa Village Tourism Association and Namegawa Village Board of Education) : (Enshrined deities) {{ill|lt=Karaijin Ōkami|Q10892534|qid=Q10892534|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} : (Founded) {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|qid=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 2 (1395) * Mizubusa, Namegawa Town – Awasu ShrineFrom the shrine notice board of Awasu Shrine (Created May 2005 by Namegawa Town Tourism Association and Namegawa Town Board of Education) : (Enshrined deities) {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Ōtomowake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} : (Founded) during the {{ill|lt=Einin|Q1327307|qid=Q1327307|1=Einin}} era (1293–1298) at a place called Oyama-no-dai. Relocated to the present site in {{ill|lt=Kan’ei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 1 (1624). : (Notes) Enshrined by invitation from {{ill|lt=Izakawa Awa Shrine|Q135098820|qid=Q135098820|1=Isagawa Awa Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|qid=Q7553491|1=Soekami District, Nara}} (now a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Izakawa Shrine|Q22117854|qid=Q22117854|1=Isagawa Shrine}}) * Tarōmaru, Ranzan Town – Awazu ShrineFrom Saitama Prefecture “Shrine Register” (Home Ministry, 1878), Awazu Shrine, Tarōmaru, Nanagō Village : (Enshrined deity) Hayamitamahime no Mikoto : (Founded) Genroku 8 (1695) : (Notes) A divided spirit from the Awazu Myōjin Shrine of Mizubusa Village (present-day Awasu Shrine) * Katsuta, Ranzan Town – Awazu Shrine : (Enshrined deities) {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Ōtomowake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} : (Founded) during the {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|qid=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} era (1704–1711) === Shrine rank === * {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|qid=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 14 (1729), September 29 – Promoted to Senior First Rank * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1874) – Gōsha : Parishioners from Mizubusa Village were added to those of Iko no Hayamitamahime Shrine, making it a Gōsha. “Namegawa Village History: Folklore Volume” (Publisher: Namegawa Village, October 1984) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 (1912), April 2 – Designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and ritual implements === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} (おきながたらしひめのみこと) (Empress Jingū) * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Ōtomowake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (おおともわけのみこと) (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} (たけしうちのすくね) === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Hachiman Shrine : The Hachiman Shrine formerly located in the area called Gōshamae was merged with the precinct Atago Shrine and renamed to its current shrine name. * Kotohira Shrine * Tenman Tenjin Shrine File:Iko-jinja Hachimangu.jpg|Hachiman Shrine File:Iko-jinja Konpira-jinja.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Iko-jinja Tenmanten-jinja.jpg|Tenman Tenjin Shrine === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Tōbu Tōjō Line|Q11527536|qid=Q11527536|1=Tōbu Tōjō Lines}} {{ill|lt=Shinrin-Kōen Station (Saitama)|Q5368656|qid=Q5368656|lt=Shinrin-Kōen Station|1=Shinrin-kōen Station (Saitama)}}, take the Kokusai Jūō Kōtsū bus bound for “Kumagaya Station South Exit.” Get off at the “Shinrin-Kōen South Exit” bus stop, then walk 30 minutes (2.8 km). === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 194, Hiki District No. 9|chapter=Iko Village|publisher=Home Ministry Geographic Bureau|date=June 1884|id={{NDLJP|764006/88}}|ref={{sfnref|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki}}}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Iko-no-hayamitama Hime-no Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iko-no-hayamitama Hime-no Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines
